item.description: A philosopher and M.P. argues that the financial and commercial interdependence of modern nations renders military conquest economically futile and traditional empire-building self-defeating.
item.source:markdown: A philosopher and M.P. argues that the financial and commercial interdependence of modern nations renders military conquest economically futile and traditional empire-building self-defeating.

item.title: The Sword of Damocles, by Anna Katharine Green
item.description: A successful New York businessman, burdened with a dark secret in his past, is threatened with exposure and ruin.
item.source:markdown: A successful New York businessman, burdened with a dark secret in his past, is threatened with exposure and ruin.

item.title: Moving the Mountain, by Charlotte Perkins Gilman
item.description: A man recovers his awareness after thirty years abroad suffering from a head injury and returns home to find society utterly changed at its most fundamental levels.
item.source:markdown: A man recovers his awareness after thirty years abroad suffering from a head injury and returns home to find society utterly changed at its most fundamental levels.

item.title: Banjo, by Claude McKay
item.description: A band of beachcombers join together to live as vagrants in Marseilles.
item.source:markdown: A band of beachcombers join together to live as vagrants in Marseilles.

Bel Ami, by Guy de Maupassant
item.description: A former soldier seduces and manipulates women in order to rise through Parisian society.
item.source:markdown: A former soldier seduces and manipulates women in order to rise through Parisian society.

item.description: A Syrian Arab’s fortunes rise and fall as he wanders away from his home by the sea.
item.source:markdown: A Syrian Arab’s fortunes rise and fall as he wanders away from his home by the sea.

item.description: A group of men hunt for the kidnappers of a friend’s wife.
item.source:markdown: A group of men hunt for the kidnappers of a friend’s wife.

item.title: Sailing Alone Around the World, by Joshua Slocum
item.description: A sailor gives his account how he completed the first solo sailing voyage around the world.
item.source:markdown: A sailor gives his account how he completed the first solo sailing voyage around the world.

item.title: The Fifth Queen Crowned, by Ford Madox Ford
item.description: Katharine Howard, Queen of England, reaches the height of her power, only to find that idealism and integrity are fragile weapons against court intrigue and religious faction.
item.source:markdown: Katharine Howard, Queen of England, reaches the height of her power, only to find that idealism and integrity are fragile weapons against court intrigue and religious faction.

item.description: A scandal that threatens the future of an exclusive boys’ school poses a moral dilemma for its headmaster.

item.title: The Nature of a Crime, by Joseph Conrad and Ford Madox Ford
item.description: After having gambled away assets entrusted to him, a lawyer writes a series of letters to his lover in an attempt to unburden his conscience.
item.source:markdown: After having gambled away assets entrusted to him, a lawyer writes a series of letters to his lover in an attempt to unburden his conscience.
